purpose: Plan a small future promotion path from a research seed into governed doctrine, concept, application, claim, and relationship objects.

candidate_new_nodes.description: Concept node for personhood, relation, communion, agency, and non-reductive human identity.
candidate_new_nodes.promotion_condition: Create only if no adequate personhood node already exists.

candidate_new_nodes.description: Concept node for human moral agency, responsibility, decision ownership, and accountable participation.
candidate_new_nodes.promotion_condition: Create only after confirming relationship to concept.vocation and concept.stewardship.

candidate_new_nodes.description: A prose bridge showing the derivation from Trinity to personhood to anthropology to AI governance constraints.
candidate_new_nodes.promotion_condition: Best first promoted file after review.

candidate_relationship_objects.notes: Needs careful review to avoid over-modernizing patristic trinitarian language.
Likely useful for graph traversal after personhood node exists.
Could become a claim object before becoming a relationship object.
Requires checking whether doctrine.sin exists and how fallenness is currently modeled.

implementation_sequence: Check existing concept and application nodes to avoid duplicates.
Promote a single prose bridge file first if review passes.
Create at most one concept node in the next Codex pass if missing: concept.personhood.
Create claim objects only after the prose bridge and concept vocabulary stabilize.
Create relationship objects only after relationship_type vocabulary review.
guardrails: Do not promote incoming research automatically.
Do not overwrite existing doctrine nodes wholesale.
Do not create tradition-specific claims without explicit tradition scope.
Do not mix asserted and inferred claims in the same object.
Do not create graph objects before checking relationship registry and existing patterns.
review_questions: Should Christology be an explicit intermediate node in this slice?
Which relationship_type names already exist and should be reused?
Which claims are source-supported enough for proposed claim objects?
Which claims are prudential governance applications rather than direct doctrinal assertions?
